tools.name: extract_pdf
tools.description: Extract a PDF into clean Markdown and structured elements (headings, paragraphs). Returns the canonical ExtractedDocument object. mode "hybrid" runs a heavier semantic engine that also reconstructs tables and bounding boxes; the default "fast" engine is prose-only (low confidence.tables).

tools.name: get_chunks
tools.description: Split a PDF into semantic chunks ready for embeddings (RAG). Each chunk carries its text, estimated tokens, starting page, section heading and the source element ids for citation.

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: Return every table in a PDF as structured JSON (reconstructed rows and columns) with page and bounding box for verifiable citations. Uses the semantic (hybrid) engine.

tools.name: create_doc_link
tools.description: Publish a PDF as a tracked doc.page Doc Link and get back a shareable URL. The link belongs to the API key's account and also appears in its doc.page library. Requires an API key. Free plan: up to 3 active links; custom vanity slugs are premium-only. Optional expiry and open-notification toggle.

tools.name: list_doc_links
tools.description: List the Doc Links of the API key's account (id, slug, URL, name, disabled/expiry state, total views, last view). Use this to recover links created in earlier sessions before querying stats. Requires an API key.

tools.name: get_doc_link_stats
tools.description: Reading analytics for one Doc Link of the API key's account, by id or slug. Always returns the summary (total views, unique visitors, last visit). Premium plans additionally get countries, visitor companies (as_org) and per-page views + average dwell time; pass include:["visits"] for the recent visi

tools.name: revoke_doc_link
tools.description: Disable a Doc Link of the API key's account (by id or slug) so the public URL stops serving. The item and its stats remain in the library; on the free plan this frees an active-link slot. Requires an API key.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_doc_link, list_doc_links · Private data: get_chunks, create_doc_link, list_doc_links, get_doc_link_stats, revoke_doc_link · Public sink: create_doc_link.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_doc_link, list_doc_links · Private data: get_chunks, create_doc_link, list_doc_links, get_doc_link_stats, revoke_doc_link · Public sink: create_doc_link

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
